definition: Systematically identify and solve the single constraint limiting business growth; visibility into operational constraints enables strategic focus.
rationale: Centroid is too specific/axis-less. Members cohere around identifying constraints (operational, self-awareness, margin visibility) as prerequisite to solving them.

definition: Diagnose whether business lacks customers (demand) or capacity (supply); misdiagnosis leads to wrong tactics.
rationale: Tight semantic cluster; four distinct phrasings of the same concept; min_intra_sim 0.917 supports cohesion.

splits.definition: Stack multiple offer tiers with distinct pricing; high-margin premium funds low-margin volume.
Profit concentrates at high-price tiers; margin improvement beats revenue growth in fixed-reimbursement models.

definition: Multiple pricing models and margin strategies for business scaling.
rationale: Cluster conflates tiering strategy (offer architecture) with margin concentration (where profit lives). Split by semantic boundary: pricing design vs. margin analysis.

definition: Clarity and language beat razzle-dazzle in content; clear CTAs, thumbnails, and messaging drive action over production quality.
rationale: Seven-member cluster cohesive on message clarity as differentiator; min_intra_sim 0.907 confirms tight grouping.

definition: Base competence and mastery require sustained volume of deliberate actions; 100x repetition breaks through plateaus.
rationale: Centroid 'Rule of 100' is catchphrase; members cohere around repetition/volume (20-hour baseline, 100x actions, hundreds of hours, skill compounding) as prerequisites for depth.

definition: Jump price 5x, not 50%, to create distinct market tiers.
rationale: Only one of three members (pricing jumps) coheres with canonical. Equity/stakes is financial structure; university exclusivity is partnership strategy — both orthogonal to pricing design. Kick outliers; keep only pricing-relevant member.

splits.definition: Guide members through four milestones (Connect→Refer→Testify→Ascend) with transparent process and touchpoints.
Scale teams through systematic training (Document-Demonstrate-Duplicate) and scripted processes (boiler room rotation, core four methods).

definition: Member and team progression through lifecycle and training.
rationale: Cluster splits on lifecycle orchestration (member journey transparency) vs. training/scaling systems (DDD method, boiler room rotation). Different load-bearing claims.

definition: Recruit independent businesses (affiliates, coaches, agencies) to sell and promote; highest-leverage lead getter at scale.
rationale: Six-member cluster cohesive on leveraging partners as lead and revenue multipliers; min_intra_sim 0.899 confirms tight grouping.

definition: Sustained, full commitment to one skill beats competitors because 99% quit; persistence is invisible and rare.
rationale: Four-member cluster tight on consistency/persistence as differentiator; all members articulate the same insight from different angles.

splits.definition: Avoid middle ground; choose ultra-cheap (automation-heavy) or high-touch (sophisticated operators); adjust LTV:CAC targets accordingly.

definition: Strategic choice between automation and human-in-loop delivery models.
rationale: Humanoid robots is product-specific outlier unrelated to business delivery model design. Remaining members cohere on barbell strategy (cheap vs. high-touch, no middle).

definition: When operationally constrained, stopping sales masks the real problem; solve capacity before pursuing growth.
rationale: Four-member cluster cohesive on supply-side bottlenecks; min_intra_sim 0.919 confirms tight grouping; complements cluster 87 (demand vs. supply diagnosis).

definition: Scale sales from founder-driven to multiplier role; train teams with role-play and process; directors build culture, not close deals.
rationale: Seven-member cluster cohesive on founder-to-leader transition; identity shift, training, process structure all support sales team multiplication.

definition: AI-driven systems enhance lead nurturing, engagement, and conversion.
rationale: All eight members exhibit low-quality extraction: same boilerplate prefix, vague headlines, no load-bearing claims (no mechanism, no conditions, no specificity). Fails NODE-QUALITY-RUBRIC §A2 (feature list masquerading as claim) and §A7 (self-referential definition). Recommend re-extraction.

definition: Max out proven channels before opening new ones; improve existing business before pivoting.
rationale: Seven-member cluster cohesive on optimization-first strategy; themes (acquisition channels, market dominance, leverage more) consistently oppose premature expansion.

definition: Stack multiple tiers with escalating price, complexity, and personalization; each tier unlocks prerequisite knowledge for next.
rationale: Five-member cluster cohesive on tiering as stacking mechanism (book series, career ladder, bonus tiers, custom orders); emphasis is progression, not just offering choice.
